records.ety: [F. embarrasser (cf. Sp. embarazar, Pg. embaraçar, Pr. barras bar); pref. em- (L. in) + LL. barra bar. See Bar.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Embarrassed (m*br"rst); p. pr. & vb. n. Embarrassing.]

defs: To hinder from freedom of thought, speech, or action by something which impedes or confuses mental action; to make (a person) unpleasantly self-conscious; to perplex; to discompose; to disconcert; as, laughter may embarrass an orator.
To hinder from liberty of movement; to impede; to obstruct; as, business is embarrassed; public affairs are embarrassed.

To involve in difficulties concerning money matters; to incumber with debt; to beset with urgent claims or demands; -- said of a person or his affairs; as, a man or his business is embarrassed when he can not meet his pecuniary engagements.
synonyms: Perplex. We are puzzled when our faculties are confused by something we do not understand. We are perplexed when our feelings
are so affected that we know not how to decide or act. We are embarrassed when there is some bar or hindrance upon us which impedes our powers of thought
or motion. A schoolboy is puzzled by a difficult sum
a reasoner is perplexed by the subtleties of his opponent
a youth is sometimes so embarrassed before strangers as to lose his presence of mind
